response.original_title: Señoritas limeñas exhiben modelos: Acontencimiento social en beneficio de las Asociación de Artistas Aficionados
response.overview: A 1940s newsreel shows young women from Lima modeling elegant Vogue fashions, with makeup and hair done by the Elizabeth Arden Beauty Institute, and carrying fine handbags from Pedro P. Díaz's Fábrica América. The event took place at the renowned Embassy venue, benefiting the Asociación de Artistas Aficionados (Association of Amateur Artists), and was attended by prominent figures from Lima society. At the end of the screening, Mrs. Elvira Miró Quesada de Roca Rey, representing the Association, provided details of the gathering. The event concluded with a dance accompanied by live music. A view of Plaza San Martín (Lima) and its surroundings can be seen at the end of the film.
response.title: Señoritas limeñas exhiben modelos: Acontencimiento social en beneficio de las Asociación de Artistas Aficionados
